seo_description: Current time in Muscat, Oman. Gulf Standard Time (GST, UTC+4) year-round — no DST. Same time as Dubai & Abu Dhabi. Live clock.

faq.question: What time is it in Muscat right now?
faq.answer: Muscat, Oman uses Gulf Standard Time (GST, UTC+4) year-round. Oman does not observe Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is Asia/Muscat. Muscat shares the same time zone as Dubai and Abu Dhabi (UAE) — both use UTC+4 year-round. The live clock above shows the current Muscat time.

faq.question: What time zone is Muscat in?
faq.answer: Muscat, Oman is in the Gulf Standard Time zone (GST, UTC+4) year-round. The IANA identifier is Asia/Muscat. Oman does not observe Daylight Saving Time, so the UTC+4 offset is constant throughout the year. Muscat shares this time zone with Dubai, Abu Dhabi, and the rest of the UAE.

faq.question: Does Muscat observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Muscat and Oman do not observe Daylight Saving Time. Oman uses GST (UTC+4) permanently year-round. The Gulf region, including UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Bahrain, and Kuwait, does not observe DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Muscat and London?
faq.answer: Muscat (GST, UTC+4) is 4 hours ahead of London (GMT, UTC+0) in winter. During British Summer Time (BST, UTC+1), Muscat is 3 hours ahead of London. Oman does not observe DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Muscat and New York?
faq.answer: Muscat (GST, UTC+4) is 9 hours ahead of New York (EST, UTC-5) in winter. During US DST (EDT, UTC-4), Muscat is 8 hours ahead. Oman does not observe DST.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Muscat?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Muscat are 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM GST, Sunday through Thursday (Friday and Saturday are the weekend). Muscat is the capital and largest city of Oman. Oman's economy is driven by oil and gas, tourism, and logistics — the Port of Salalah is one of the world's busiest container ports. Muscat Securities Market (MSM) trades Sunday through Thursday.
